decision.semantic_admission_delta: positive for focused same-file direct-function and DirectMethod branch-return Promise producer fixtures; unchanged on the local crates runtime surface because crates has no JS/TS Promise continuation rows
decision.summary: Extend the proof-backed local Promise producer recovery from single-return bodies to branch-return bodies when every returned expression has asserted PromiseLike domain evidence. The value graph recovers only same-channel Promise states through branch Phi values and remains closed for mixed fulfillment/rejection branches.
policy: A direct or member call result used as a Promise continuation receiver is not Promise proof by itself.
Branch-return recovery requires existing DirectFunction or DirectMethod target evidence plus PromiseLike Domain evidence for every returned expression on the supported return paths.
The value graph evaluates the supported body through the existing pure-inline sink fence; this does not open generic helper inlining.

Fulfilled/fulfilled and rejected/rejected branch Promise states can recover as a same-channel Phi; fulfilled/rejected mixed branches remain closed because they require explicit settlement-channel semantics.
Imported function/member receivers remain closed because import target coordinates do not provide an evaluable body; they still require a settled-value contract.

corpus_source_pricing.scan_condition: 120-repo JS/TS lexical source scan for .then/.catch/.finally call-return receivers

priced_receiver_classes.opened_subset_before_this_slice: same-file direct function callees with a single returned expression carrying PromiseLike domain proof
priced_receiver_classes.opened_subset_after_this_slice: same-file direct function callees with supported branch-return bodies where every returned expression carries PromiseLike domain proof

priced_receiver_classes.opened_subset_before_this_slice: existing DirectMethod target records with a single returned expression carrying PromiseLike domain proof
priced_receiver_classes.opened_subset_after_this_slice: existing DirectMethod target records with supported branch-return bodies where every returned expression carries PromiseLike domain proof and the body does not read receiver context

limitations: The source scan prices the broader receiver queues, not branch-return prevalence specifically.
The local/parameter bucket still mixes direct local functions and parameter or dynamic function values; this slice opens only direct local functions.
The member bucket still requires DirectMethod evidence from the frontend or semantic packs; selector-only member calls remain closed.
A returned Promise.resolve argument must still be non-thenable-safe before fulfillment recovery opens.

kernel_capabilities: call-target result-domain evidence can now depend on all supported returned expressions rather than only one single-return expression
direct function and DirectMethod Promise producer recovery evaluate the supported body through the existing pure-inline sink fence
Promise boundary recovery understands Phi values only when both branch states share the same channel
value-domain propagation preserves same-domain Phi payloads so branch payload continuations retain non-thenable-safe domain proof
newly_supported_equivalences: function load(flag) { if (flag) return Promise.resolve(1); return Promise.resolve(2); } load(flag).then(x => x + 1) converges with Promise.resolve(flag ? 1 : 2).then(x => x + 1)
the analogous DirectMethod branch-return producer can feed local Promise continuation recovery when DirectMethod target evidence is present
recovered branch-return Promise continuations stay distinct from synchronous payload code
still_closed_equivalences: branches that mix Promise.resolve and Promise.reject stay closed until settlement-channel semantics are explicit
branch-return producers missing PromiseLike domain proof on any returned expression stay closed
DirectMethod branch-return bodies that read this, super, or self stay closed
parameter callees, dynamic dispatch, imported function/member call returns, constructors, .finally, aggregate combinators, await, and broad scheduling remain closed
reporting_delta: The Promise continuation recall-loss fixture now includes a branch-return direct producer and still does not report promise-call-return-direct-function-return-domain-proof for supported same-file direct producers.

The newly added branch receiver still reports promise-then-fulfillment-continuation-contract-missing when the callback itself lacks continuation proof; producer recovery does not bypass callback obligations.

observed_promise_delta: promise-then-fulfillment-continuation-contract-missing count is 3 after adding thenBranched, because arbitrary callback continuation proof remains required
promise-call-return-direct-function-return-domain-proof is not reported for supported same-file direct Promise-returning producers

covered: DirectFunction branch-return bodies emit call-result Domain(PromiseLike) only when every returned expression has PromiseLike domain evidence
DirectMethod branch-return bodies emit call-result Domain(PromiseLike) only from existing DirectMethod target evidence plus returned-expression domain evidence
same-channel fulfilled branch-return producers recover through a Promise Phi and feed .then fulfillment
mixed fulfilled/rejected branch-return producers stay closed
branch-return Promise.resolve producers converge with direct conditional Promise.resolve continuations
branch-return Promise continuations do not collapse into synchronous payload equivalence
mixed fulfilled/rejected branch-return producers do not converge with direct branch continuations
supported branch-return direct producers are not left behind as missing direct-function return-domain proof
remaining work is still attributed to continuation/channel/callback obligations

next_work: Add a structured source scan that distinguishes single-return and branch-return Promise producers inside the existing local/parameter and member receiver queues.
Design the minimal settled-value evidence capability for imported Promise producers instead of inferring from import target coordinates.
Keep mixed settlement-channel branch recovery closed until the scheduling/channel model can represent fulfilled versus rejected branch obligations explicitly.
